description_long: This is a classic black rotary dial corded telephone, featuring a robust rectangular base with rounded edges and a glossy finish. The handset is large and ergonomically curved, resting securely on a cradle at the top of the base, and is connected to the main unit by a coiled cord. The rotary dial is prominently positioned on the sloped front face, with circular finger holes and visible numbers for dialing. The base appears to be made of a hard plastic or Bakelite material, with metallic internal components likely present for the rotary mechanism and wiring. The phone's design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing durability and function. This type of phone was commonly used in homes and offices throughout the mid-20th century.
description: A vintage black rotary dial corded telephone with a coiled cord and a glossy, robust base. The handset rests on a cradle, and the rotary dial is prominently displayed on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, cradle, and coiled cord attached to the handset.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the base and the profile of the handset resting on the cradle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handset and the smooth, unadorned back of the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the coiled cord attachment and the rotary dial from an angle.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
